summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a legendary Fillmore East night – smoking Hard to Handle, epic Alligator → Cold Rain & Snow jam and standout Bird Song.
review: The 4‑29‑71 Fillmore East recording is crystal‑clear (soundboard) and lets the band’s fire shine through.  Highlights include a blistering Hard to Handle, an unforgettable Alligator/Cold Rain & Snow sequence, soaring Bird Song, and powerful renditions of Morning Dew, Ripple and Midnight Hour.  Even the occasional low‑energy moment can’t dim what many fans call one of the era’s most essential shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 % of reviews are 5‑star, praising both performance and audio; a handful of lower scores mention minor energy or technical glitches, yielding an overall weighted rating in the high‑4 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the mix A+/A++, noting crisp instruments, vivid drum solo and no audible flaws; a few mention occasional low energy but nothing that detracts from clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds with Hard to Handle and tight playing; second set launches into a massive Alligator jam that morphs into Cold Rain & Snow, then rolls through Bird Song, Morning Dew and the soaring Midnight Hour – a seamless, high‑impact arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his “turn‑out” tone, razor‑sharp riffs on Hard to Handle, soaring solos on Bird Song and overall inspiring guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lauded for his “pure groove” bass, “bombs” during jams, and the deep foundation he provides throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for Pig Pen’s charismatic vocals, humor (Bobby’s laugh after Truckin’), and strong organ contributions on several numbers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo between Alligator and the jam is described as “in your living room”; his spoken rap before Midnight Hour also receives fan love.
